resumeRag.title: Resume Questions
resumeRag.subtitle: Answers are grounded in resume evidence loaded into the DB.
resumeRag.empty: Ask a question and the answer will appear here.
resumeRag.emptyTitle: What would you like to know?
resumeRag.emptyDescription: Start with a suggested question or type your own.
resumeRag.introTitle: Check this resume quickly through questions
resumeRag.introDescription: Ask about role fit, key projects, technical strengths, and working style.

readmeEntry.title: Have a question while reading the README?
readmeEntry.description: I will find resume evidence and prepare an answer.
readmeEntry.placeholder: Ask what you want to know while reading the README.
readmeEntry.submittingHint: Searching resume evidence and preparing an answer.
readmeEntry.readyHint: The answer is ready. Open the question page to see the conversation.

suggestions: What did you work on at Oprimed?
What is your CI/CD and deployment experience?

questionTopics.title: Role fit
questionTopics.description: Check experience and strengths for the position.
questions: Explain why you are a fit for frontend product development roles.
What are your strengths across healthcare, commerce, and back-office work?
What evidence shows you can work as a product-minded developer?

questionTopics.title: Key projects
questionTopics.description: Check real problems solved and the role taken.
questions: Describe your most representative project and your role.
Summarize the problem you solved and your contribution at Oprimed.
What outcomes came from your operations tools or automation projects?

questionTopics.title: Technical strengths
questionTopics.description: Check frontend, testing, and deployment experience.
questions: How have you used React and Next.js in real work?
Describe your testing and deployment automation experience.
How have you handled state management and API integration?

questionTopics.title: Working style
questionTopics.description: Check planning, quality, automation, and collaboration.
questions: How do you clarify ambiguous requirements?
What have you done to improve quality and maintainability?
Tell me about a case where you automated repetitive work.

questionTopics.title: Expanded career evidence
questionTopics.description: Check concrete evidence for performance, shared UI, data recovery, browser behavior, and product analytics.
questions: Explain the build-time image optimization and transfer-size reduction evidence.
Explain the scope of the shared UI and design-token migration.
How did you handle task-scoped caching and result restoration?
How did you apply GA4 behavioral analytics events at Code Crayon?
How did you handle Safari and WebKit differences in a mobile WebView?
How did you prevent duplicate input and control state transitions during game result submission?

pending.title: Searching evidence and generating with Codex
pending.description: The API is retrieving public resume evidence from the DB and generating an answer.

pendingHints: I worked on input screens, result screens, charts, and data revisit flows for a medical analytics product.
I moved subtitle translation work from CLI scripts into a web tool operators could use.
I built a mobile webview area for running events and game content without app releases.
I turned repeated manual searches for copied content into a crawling-based checking tool.
I worked on both customer-facing commerce screens and back-office operation screens.
I connected type checks and builds so failed changes could be caught before they were pushed.
I think about ways to reduce human error.
For repetitive work, I look for ways to turn it into a system.
I ask whether I would use it if I were the user.

noEvidence.title: Not enough evidence
noEvidence.description: The retrieved public resume evidence is insufficient, so confidence is shown as low.

origin-blocked.title: Request is not allowed
origin-blocked.description: This question API only accepts requests sent from the official VSCoke production site.

auth-required.title: Login required
auth-required.description: Resume questions require an authenticated session.

auth-expired.title: Session expired
auth-expired.description: Refresh your session and ask again.

service-unavailable.title: Answer generation is not ready
service-unavailable.description: Codex app-server, embedding settings, or vector indexing needs attention.

rate-limited.title: Request limit reached
rate-limited.description: Wait briefly, then retry the same question.

contract.title: Unexpected response shape
contract.description: The API response contract does not match what this screen expects.

request.title: Failed to request an answer
request.description: Check the network or API state, then try again.

resume.name: Sangmin Lee
introduction: I have developed commerce and medical and clinical analysis products, building both the screens users experience and the tools operators use.
I am extending AI use beyond individual productivity into the team's development and operational practices.

oprimed.achievement: Developed medical and clinical analysis product screens and data flows while also leading team operations and deployment and verification standards.

medicalFrontendProductization.title: Medical and Clinical Analysis Product Screens and Analysis Data

descriptions.subtitle: Medical and Clinical Analysis Product Screens and Analysis Data
descriptions.detail: Developed input and result screens and data save and retrieval flows for a medical and clinical analysis product.
descriptions.skills: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, TanStack Query, Zustand, React Hook Form, ECharts, Plotly, Vitest, Playwright, Sharp, GitHub Actions, Husky, Docker, Bash
tasks: Saved analysis data to the server and separated caches by task so the same results and conditions are restored after re-entry or refresh.
Strengthened validation and error cases and processed data uploads in chunks.
Replaced image-based UI with code-based components and moved buttons, icons, and toggles to shared structures. Migrated 529 direct color codes into design tokens.
Generated static images as viewport-specific WebP files at build time and regenerated and verified only changed assets, reducing transfer size by about 95%.
Reduced Lighthouse LCP from 1.3 seconds to 1.0 seconds and Speed Index from 3.4 seconds to 0.7 seconds.

descriptions.subtitle: Team Operations and Development and Verification Standards
descriptions.detail: Alongside frontend development, handled scheduling, work allocation, code reviews, and one-on-ones while establishing development and verification standards the team could reuse.
tasks: Directly supported blocked implementation work and high-schedule-risk areas.
Replaced FTP-based manual deployments, which were slow and location-bound, with GitHub Actions CI/CD.
Unified execution and deployment specifications across 4 frontend projects that had different conventions and frameworks.

Automated core user-flow verification with Playwright E2E tests across Chrome, Edge, Safari (WebKit), and key viewport sizes, and used Husky pre-push checks to block typecheck and build failures from reaching remote branches.
Distributed project guidance and concept documents to the team and connected them with repository skills, hooks, and PR rules so the team could develop and review against the same standards.

descriptions.subtitle: Customer-facing services and operator tools
descriptions.detail: As an early member, developed both customer-facing services and operator tools.
descriptions.skills: Next.js, React, TypeScript, MUI, Tailwind CSS, Jotai, SWR, React Hook Form, i18next, GA4, Google Tag Manager, Google Sheets API
tasks: Developed product, order, payment, gifting, draw, and account flows for a multilingual commerce service, and connected view, favorite, signup, and purchase actions to GA4 and GTM events.
Collected translation keys for four languages from code and synchronized Google Sheets with translation files in both directions.
Migrated a Flutter Web back office to Next.js and developed product and seller approval, order and claim, settlement, and inquiry flows.
Applied search reset, CSV and Excel export, drag sorting, and shared table and dialog components.

subtitleSystem.title: AI Subtitle Translation and Content Operations Automation

descriptions.subtitle: Moved repetitive operations into web tools
descriptions.detail: Turned repetitive operational work into web tools that operators continue to use.
descriptions.skills: Node.js, TypeScript, Express, AWS Bedrock, OpenAI API, Google Cloud Translation, Axios, Cheerio, Vercel Serverless Functions
tasks: Moved subtitle translation from a CLI to a web management tool so operators could handle files, prompts, review, and character matching.
Applied chunked and parallel translation, retry handling, and checks for subtitle counts, omissions, and timing.
Changed translation throughput from 3 people handling 1-2 works a day to 1 person handling 6-8 works a day; the system is still in use.
Moved browser-blocked search to a Node.js server and automated searches across three platforms with similar-title filtering, reducing a daily check from 50 minutes to under 10 minutes.

descriptions.subtitle: An independently deployable content-update structure
descriptions.detail: Designed and developed a Playground from the start so events and games could be updated independently of app releases.
descriptions.skills: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Phaser, Next Intl, Flutter InAppWebView, Web Audio API, Airbridge, Branch.io
tasks: Separated events, fortune content, games, and draws into domain units so content could be added independently of app releases.
Connected Flutter WebView communication, deep links, and reward ads, and controlled duplicate input and screen state during game-result submission.
Handled keyboard, focus, safe-area, theme, and audio differences across Chrome, Safari, and Samsung Internet.

descriptions.subtitle: Moved repetitive manual checks into a crawling-based tool
descriptions.detail: Moved unauthorized-content checking work from repetitive manual searches into a crawling-based tool.
descriptions.skills: TypeScript, Express, Axios, Cheerio, Vercel Serverless Functions
tasks: Built a keyword-based crawling tool and result-review screen.
Reduced daily checking work from 50 minutes to under 10 minutes.
Separated bot-blocked browser crawling areas into Node.js-based processing.

descriptions.subtitle: Insurance subscription screen with reduced initial display delay
descriptions.detail: Reduced a section where the insurance subscription screen could take up to 5 seconds to complete the initial display.
descriptions.skills: React, Next.js, TypeScript, Redux, Context API
tasks: Removed nested callbacks and simplified the order of data preparation, state reflection, and rendering.
Moved React-based screens into a Next.js structure and reset routing and rendering standards.
Achieved 1.2s LCP on the subscription screen.

descriptions.subtitle: Insurance subscription, payment, and admin screens
descriptions.detail: Developed insurance subscription, payment, and admin screens.

tasks: Built responsive insurance subscription screens and payment-information input flows.
Built subscription lists, detail screens, statistics charts, report download, and file upload/download features.
Shared upload/download handling and types to reduce admin-screen maintenance cost.

consularCallCenter.title: Maintenance, API Development, and Incident Response for a Live Service

descriptions.subtitle: Maintenance and development work for a live service
descriptions.detail: Performed maintenance and development work for a live service. Classified issues by user impact, reproducibility, and fix scope, then shared them with the operations team.
descriptions.skills: Linux, Network, Security, API, JavaScript, HTML, CSS
tasks: Reproduced errors and change requests, then separated the scope that needed fixes and verification.
Checked incident causes and user impact from infrastructure and security perspectives, then shared findings with the operations team.
Worked on API development and selected page implementation, gaining experience in how development and operations connect.

descriptions.detail: Worked on data conversion and environment expansion so existing software could run on different databases and runtime environments.

tasks: Adjusted Oracle-based queries and Linux-centered features to work in MariaDB and Windows environments.
Expanded the usable environments of existing software through library conversion and runtime environment expansion.
Added Windows-environment features and documentation so operators could use the transitioned environment.
